results.title: ASEAN Extradition Treaty Ratification Status and Singapore's Preparatory Steps for Implementation of Ratification
results.summary: As of the debate, no ASEAN Member State has ratified the ASEAN Treaty on Extradition adopted on 14 November 2025, and no agreed timeframe exists for its entry into force. To ratify, Singapore must update the Extradition Act notifications to extend their application to the Treaty upon its eventual entry into force. The Minister for Law confirmed these prerequisites and the current lack of ratification status among member states.

results.title: Adjournment of Debate
results.summary: Senior Minister of State Janil Puthucheary moved to adjourn the debate on the resumption of the current discussion. The Minister confirmed that the debate will resume tomorrow. No new policy measures, legislative changes, or operational constraints were introduced during this procedural motion. The item serves solely to schedule the continuation of the existing parliamentary business without altering any substantive policy positions or eligibility criteria.

results.title: Access to Essential Banking Services by Individuals under Police Investigation for Scams
results.summary: Under section 35(8) of the Criminal Procedure Code, individuals with frozen accounts due to scam investigations may apply to the Courts for fund release covering basic living expenses. The Police do not restrict access to income or essential services; affected individuals can utilize non-frozen accounts or open new ones subject to bank due diligence. Those facing application difficulties may approach the Investigation Officer or seek legal advice. Recourse beyond appealing to the Police includes these specific operational pathways for maintaining daily financial needs.

results.title: Adequacy of Adult Disability Residential Services and Support Programmes
results.summary: As of December 2025, MSF-funded Adult Disability Homes offer 1,500 places with over 80% utilisation and a 100-person waitlist, while Adult Disability Hostels provide 130 places with 75 individuals waiting. Capacity expanded by 300 places in 2022, with a new facility planned by 2030. The Enabling Living Programme launched in December 2025, and the Home Support Programme pilot begins in the second half of 2026; together, these community-based pilots will support up to 250 persons with disabilities. The Minister confirmed these figures and timelines regarding service availability and expansion plans for adults with disabilities.

results.title: Annual Grading Framework for Student Care Centres
results.summary: Currently, only Student Care Centres administering the Student Care Fee Assistance undergo mandatory audits at least every two years. These audits assess physical environment, operating hours, supervision, safety, health, hygiene, meals, emergency preparedness, and records-keeping. The resulting audit grades are publicly available on the Ministry of Social and Family Development website. The Minister did not confirm plans to implement a mandatory annual grading framework for all centres or to mandate prominent display of grades.

results.title: Assessing Adequacy of Current Cybersecurity Readiness against Evolving Threats while Ensuring Operational Security
results.summary: The Minister stated that Singapore's cybersecurity posture is robust and adaptive, with the Cyber Security Agency (CSA) regularly updating standards and obligations under the Cybersecurity Act to address evolving threats, including AI-enabled attacks. Critical systems face higher standards, while GovTech will introduce stricter cybersecurity and data protection obligations for Government vendors managing sensitive data, requiring them to meet Cyber Trust Mark requirements. To assist businesses, CSA launched the CISO-as-a-Service programme for SMEs and will raise mandatory cybersecurity requirements for home routers from Cyber Labelling Scheme Level 1 to Level 2, with potential future standards for IP cameras. The Government will continue reviewing policies to ensure citizens remain protected against malicious actors and advanced threat actors.

results.title: Adjusting Implementation Timeline of Sustainable Aviation Fuel Levy to Mitigate Cost Pressures on Airlines and Passengers
results.summary: In response to rising fuel costs and route disruptions caused by the Middle East conflict, the Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore has deferred the Sustainable Aviation Fuel levy. The levy will now apply to tickets and services sold from 1 October 2026, covering flights departing from 1 January 2027. This adjustment aims to mitigate near-term cost pressures on airlines and passengers while balancing Singapore's longer-term decarbonisation objectives. The Government has not considered other implementation options beyond this specific deferral.

results.title: Assessing Aggregate Exposure of Singapore-domiciled Financial Institutions to US Private Credit
results.summary: The Prime Minister and Minister for Finance stated that Singapore financial institutions maintain very small exposure to US private credit. The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) regularly monitors these risk exposures as part of its supervisory oversight. This ongoing supervision includes engaging financial institutions to stress-test their balance sheets against global scenarios, such as defaults on private credit assets. Consequently, MAS has not conducted separate stress tests specifically for US private credit exposure, as existing supervisory frameworks already incorporate such global stress scenarios.

results.title: Assessment of Expected Fare Range for Upcoming Johor Bahru-Singapore RTS Link and Measures to Ensure Fare Affordability for Commuters
results.summary: Fares for the Johor Bahru-Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link will be determined commercially by the operator, RTS Operations Pte Ltd, rather than being set by the Government. The operator will announce the specific fare range in due course. Consequently, no current assessment of the expected fare range or specific affordability arrangements for Singaporeans has been stated by the Acting Minister for Transport at this time.

results.title: Assessing Effectiveness of Foreign Policy Public Education Efforts in Understanding Singapore's Strategic Interests
results.summary: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs assesses the effectiveness of its public education engagements primarily through post-session feedback, noting that an overwhelming majority of participants report enhanced understanding of Singapore's foreign policy. While specific quantitative outcome metrics or longitudinal tracking of attitude change are not explicitly detailed, the Government collects feedback to ensure content relevance and improve engagement methods across diverse segments including students, youths, businesses, and community leaders. The Minister confirmed that these sessions aim to explain foreign policy fundamentals and responses to international developments, with positive responses indicating current success in communicating strategic interests.

results.title: Assessment of Improvements and Productivity Gains in Sea-based Fish Farms and Seafood Production
results.summary: Productivity gains in Singapore's aquaculture sector reflect a combination of farm exits and operational improvements, with 24.5% of sea-based fish farms exiting and overall production declining 14% in 2024. While productivity rose from 38.6 to 40.7 tonnes per hectare, the specific number of farms closed in 2025 and the median productivity from 2023 to 2025 remain unconfirmed. The Minister stated that 2025 data will be published later this year as part of the Singapore Food Statistics 2025.

results.title: Assessing Out-of-pocket Costs for Outpatient Mental Health Treatment Across Public and Private Settings
results.summary: In 2023, patients under the Chronic Disease Management Programme (CDMP) incurred average annual post-subsidy bills of $300 to $500 for outpatient mental health treatment at public and selected private settings. These costs are partially offset by MediSave 500/700 and Flexi-MediSave schemes. To better cover these expenses, the Ministry recently increased MediSave withdrawal limits for CDMP treatments from $500/$700 to $700/$1,000 for simple and complex chronic patients, respectively. The Ministry routinely reviews financing schemes using such data to inform ongoing adjustments.

results.title: Assessment of Market Concentration in Food Delivery Sector on Commissions or Fees and Fair Competition
results.summary: Following Deliveroo's exit in March 2026, the Competition and Consumer Commission of Singapore (CCS) is monitoring the food delivery sector for anti-competitive conduct. To date, CCS has observed no systematic shifts in commissions or fees among remaining players. The agency retains the authority to investigate anti-competitive practices under the Competition Act 2004 and encourages parties with relevant feedback to approach CCS directly. No regulatory review has been announced, though CCS will act on evidence of violations.

results.title: Breakdown of Scholarship Recipients by Country of Study over Past Decade and Minimum Annual Targets for Non-English-speaking Countries
results.summary: The Public Service Commission (PSC) provided the annual breakdown of scholarship recipients by country of study since 2016, noting that over the 2023–2025 period, 49% studied locally, 30% in the UK, 15% in the USA, and 6% elsewhere. The Minister clarified that no minimum annual targets exist for studying in non-English-speaking countries because award numbers are dynamic and depend on recipient aptitude, available courses, and areas of interest. While local scholars are encouraged to pursue overseas exchanges and postgraduate studies abroad, the government aims for a diversified portfolio to bring back diverse perspectives and connections in the mid- to long term.

results.title: Assessment of Tariff-impact on Singapore's Trade Sectors following US' Section 301 Investigations
results.summary: On 11 and 12 March 2026, the US launched two Section 301 investigations covering structural excess capacity and forced labour risks, including Singapore. The US Trade Representative initially misstated Singapore's trade balance but corrected the record to reflect a US surplus of US$27 billion in 2024. While investigations remain ongoing, the Government will not speculate on outcomes or impacts, noting that the Section 122 tariff of 10% imposed since 24 February 2026 remains unchanged. Singapore will continue constructive engagements with the US, strengthen trade diversification, and assist affected firms via the Business Adaptation Grant and feedback channels through MTI and trade associations. The Government confirmed it will attend upcoming public hearings and represent local business interests.

results.title: Breakdown of Licence Violation Cases by Citizenship Status in Past Five Years
results.summary: Between 2021 and 2025, the Traffic Police detected an average of 1,300 individuals annually driving or riding without a valid licence. Of these cases, approximately 53% involved Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents, while the remaining 47% were foreigners. The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs provided these aggregate figures but did not break down the statistics by specific citizenship status for each year. No further details on enforcement actions or penalties were stated in the response.

results.title: Breakdown of Uptake of Caregiver Support Schemes in Past Five Years by Household Income and Age Groups
results.summary: The Minister stated that caregiver support schemes, including respite services, the Caregivers Training Grant (up to $400/year), and the Home Caregiving Grant (up to $600/month), are tagged to care recipients rather than caregivers. Consequently, the Ministry does not track or publish uptake data broken down by caregiver household income or age group. From 2023 to 2025, annual average usage was approximately 17,700 for respite services, 3,200 for the Training Grant, and 72,300 for the Home Caregiving Grant. Upcoming subsidy enhancements for long-term care services are also expected to help offset caregiving costs.

results.title: Causes and Follow-up Actions for March 2026 Disruptions on Singtel’s Mobile Network
results.summary: Between 16 and 18 March 2026, Singtel experienced mobile network disruptions affecting over 600,000 customers on 16 March due to a mechanical fault, with subsequent issues on 17 and 18 March attributed to a software bug and peak traffic respectively. The Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA) is investigating the incidents, confirming no evidence of cyberattacks, and assessing impacts on emergency services. While Singtel voluntarily offered one-time rebates, IMDA enforces service standards under the Telecommunications Act, requiring resolution within one hour and robust failover systems. IMDA is reviewing resilience regulations, including stress testing requirements, and will publish findings once investigations conclude, with potential enforcement actions for lapses.

results.title: Career Conversion Programme Employment Bond Requirements, Bond Termination and Participant Numbers for Past Five Years
results.summary: Workforce Singapore does not impose employment bond requirements on Career Conversion Programme participants. Consequently, no participants were subject to bonds, zero bonds were terminated during training or service, and no average termination costs or breakdown of termination reasons exist. The Minister for Manpower clarified that the premise of the questions regarding bond proportions, participant numbers, and sector-specific breakdowns is incorrect as the programme operates without such mandatory financial obligations.

results.title: Capacity Enhancements at Sengkang HDB Branch Office to Handle Workload with Closure of Hougang Branch Office
results.summary: HDB closed its Hougang Branch on 1 September 2025 to facilitate mixed commercial and residential development at Hougang Ave 10 and Hougang Central. Staff were redeployed to Sengkang Branch to increase appointment slots, while a 24-hour e-Lobby opened simultaneously at Block 806 Hougang Central equipped with self-help machines. A service ambassador stationed at the e-Lobby will assist residents until June 2026, with potential extension if needed. HDB monitors service standards via waiting times and satisfaction scores, confirming Sengkang Branch performance meets targets and remains comparable to other branches.

results.title: Checking for Illegal Marine Species at Seafood Wholesale Markets and Border Checkpoints and Illegal Trade Prevention Measures
results.summary: The National Parks Board, in collaboration with the Singapore Food Agency, Immigration and Checkpoints Authority, and international partners, enforces CITES regulations through border assessments, periodic market surveillance at seafood wholesale markets like Jurong Fishery Port, and industry engagement. Individuals or entities found trading CITES Appendix II and III species without permits face prosecution under the Endangered Species (Import and Export) Act. Penalties for corporate offenders include fines up to the higher of $1 million or the specimens' market value, imprisonment of up to six years, or both. The transcript does not specify current inspection frequencies or recent tip-off statistics.

results.title: Clarification by Acting Minister for Transport and Senior Minister of State for Finance
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport and Senior Minister of State for Finance issued a factual correction to a previous reply regarding support measures for self-employed Combi bus and limousine drivers. The transcript clarifies that the Government collaborated with the NTUC to develop these measures in 2022, correcting an earlier statement that incorrectly cited 2002. The Minister expressed willingness to continue working with the NTUC to determine future support arrangements for this group. No specific new measures, timelines, or eligibility criteria were detailed in this correction.

results.title: Collaborating with Nephrologists to Implement Peritoneal Dialysis-first Policy and Measures to Accelerate Adoption
results.summary: The Ministry of Health maintains a Peritoneal Dialysis (PD)-preferred strategy, noting that arteriovenous fistula creation remains a clinical decision. While PD is encouraged for an active lifestyle, operational impediments like equipment breakdown risks and storage constraints persist. Financing policies ensure affordability for both PD and haemodialysis without specific targeted incentives. The Ministry will advance the PD-preferred strategy through the National PD Home Support Programme to address these challenges.

results.title: Community Support for Individuals and Society to Understand Global Conflicts
results.summary: The Acting Minister-in-charge of Muslim Affairs outlined community support mechanisms to help individuals process international conflicts while maintaining social harmony. Key initiatives include the Racial and Religious Harmony Circles, which organised inter-ethnic dialogues such as the October 2024 Peace and Harmony Dialogue on Gaza and a January 2026 youth seminar on countering digital extremism. The Rahmatan Lil Alamin Foundation (RLAF), supported by MUIS, received half a million dollars from a 2024 fundraising campaign. Asatizah Solace Care, a free and confidential counselling service initiated by PERGAS, is available to all Singaporeans, not just the Malay/Muslim community, for emotional and spiritual guidance. The Government employs a multi-level approach involving Parliament, safe-space dialogues, and grassroots Harmony Circles to address complex issues holistically. Religious teachers receive continuing education training under the Asatizah Recognition Scheme to better support the community, while collaborations with MMOs and IMOs are encouraged to jointly address societal impacts.

results.title: Community-based Interventions and Alternative Penalties for Illegal Bird-feeding Cases Involving Seniors
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that NParks partners with the Agency for Integrated Care and Town Councils to engage elderly offenders, addressing underlying motivations and misconceptions regarding illegal bird feeding. To complement enforcement, NParks plans to revise penalties to include community-based orders, expanding sentencing options for the Court. These measures target seniors aged 65 and above, who constitute approximately half of the 320 annual cases reported between 2023 and 2025. The approach aims to raise awareness on negative impacts while providing support for vulnerable residents with mental health needs.

results.title: Comparison of Rental Costs Relative to Revenue and Gross Operating Surplus in Retail and F&B Sectors between SMEs and Non-SMEs from 2019 to 2024
results.summary: The Government provided data on total annual rental costs, operating revenue, and gross operating surplus for the retail and F&B sectors from 2019 to 2024. However, a breakdown comparing these indicators between SMEs and non-SMEs is not available. The Minister noted that firms in properties with attributes like proximity to key transport nodes or high-density estates may face higher rents. No specific data on rental pressure concentration in particular property types or locations was supplied.

results.title: Competitive Strategies to Maintain Singapore as Preferred Marine Insurance Hub
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport directed Members to refer to Ministerial Statements delivered on 7 April 2026 regarding Singapore's marine war-risk insurance stake in the Arabian Gulf and strategies to maintain hub status against cheaper competitors. These statements, presented by the Deputy Prime Minister, Minister for Trade and Industry, Acting Minister for Transport, Senior Minister of State for Finance, and Coordinating Minister for National Security, also addressed the global competitiveness of tax concessions for ship-leasing, marine insurance, and shipping management. Specific details on these strategies and concession rates were not elaborated in the current transcript.

results.title: Completion Rate of Cognitive Behavioural Training of Allied Health Professionals in COMIT and Targeted Timeline for Full Training Coverage
results.summary: Approximately 45% of Community Intervention Teams (COMIT) healthcare professionals have completed Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT) training as of now, with the remainder expected to finish within the next two years. All COMIT staff commenced additional CBT training at higher learning institutes in 2024 to ensure competence in managing mental health needs. The Ministry has set a target for full training coverage across all professionals within this two-year window, aligning with the requirement for all staff to be competent in mental health management.

results.title: Comprehensive Energy Policy Response to Geopolitical Crisis Including Conservation, Diversification, Strategic Reserves and Targeted Household Assistance
results.summary: On 7 April 2026, Ministers addressed energy security concerns arising from the Middle East conflict, confirming Singapore maintains sufficient strategic stockpiles to buffer supply disruptions while assessing impacts on petrochemical sectors. The Government evaluated tailored conservation messages for lower-income households and considered specific triggers for additional financial support, though no mandatory energy-saving measures were announced. Discussions included the scope of the Committee Against Profiteering regarding utility price increases and the Singapore-Australia partnership's role in securing LNG flows. Ministers noted ongoing progress in diversifying energy and food imports but did not disclose specific quantitative targets for bilateral oil flows or exact price thresholds for household assistance.

results.title: Concern Over Surge in Million-dollar HDB Resale Flat Transactions
results.summary: In 2025, million-dollar HDB resale transactions surged 54.7% to 1,594 units, though overall price growth moderated to 2.9% with Q1 2026 flash estimates showing a slight 0.1% decline. The Minister noted that about 6% of resale flats exceeded $1 million, while options below $600,000 remain available in areas like Sembawang and Yishun. Eligible first-time buyers receive grants up to $230,000. The Government expects market moderation due to robust new Build-To-Order supply and increased flats entering the resale market from 2026 onward. A review of income eligibility criteria for BTOs and executive condominiums is ongoing, though specific impacts on resale price inflation were not explicitly detailed.

results.title: Constraints Limiting Conversion of Under-utilised HDB Multi-storey Car Park Spaces for Community Uses
results.summary: The Housing and Development Board (HDB) will not convert multi-storey car park (MSCP) spaces at Clementi Peaks for community uses because the estate was completed in July 2022 and parking needs have not yet stabilised. HDB assesses conversion requests based on resident parking sufficiency, technical feasibility regarding structural loading, and regulatory compliance including fire safety and exit capacity, explicitly excluding revenue considerations. While MSCP spaces are incidental and not designed for community activities, HDB may consider repurposing suitable lots if they can meet future parking demand and address technical constraints. Members of Parliament seeking clear criteria for change-of-use appeals should note that HDB evaluates each proposal against these specific operational and safety benchmarks rather than a fixed set of rules.

results.title: Consumer Payment Protection through Legislation of Mandatory Cash Acceptance during Digital Banking Service Failures
results.summary: The Minister referred the Member to Minister Chee Hong Tat's reply dated 26 February 2026 regarding the timeline for mandating cash acceptance by law during digital banking failures. No new timeline or legislative decision was announced in this session. The matter remains pending as per the previous response.

results.title: Contamination Rates at Household Recycling Bins and Chutes and Measures to Reduce Contamination
results.summary: The aggregated contamination rate for household recycling bins and chutes under the National Recycling Programme has remained at about 40% since 2018. The National Environment Agency is addressing this through sustained public education, such as the Recycle Right campaign, and by expanding clean stream recyclable collection via extended producer responsibility schemes for e-waste and beverage containers. The Ministry welcomes community-led initiatives to explore alternative recycling approaches and will review their effectiveness. While no specific reduction target has been set, the agency aims to reduce contamination rates as much as possible while monitoring ongoing efforts to improve recycling outcomes.

results.title: Correlation between Closure of High-profile Retail Stores and Rental Trends
results.summary: The Minister clarified that while macro-level rental costs as a share of total business costs declined from 26% to 17% for food and beverage and 30% to 26% for retail between 2019 and 2024, local variations persist due to location, property type, and demand. He noted that retailers also face other cost pressures like raw materials and labour, which may lead to restructuring or exit. To support local retailers and SMEs, the Government offers schemes including the Enterprise Development Grant and Productivity Solutions Grant to enhance productivity. Additionally, targeted programmes such as the Retail Maverick Challenge encourage innovation and differentiated offerings. The Government continues to engage with industry stakeholders and trade associations like the Singapore Retailers Association to assess and enhance support for enterprises during these transitions.

results.title: Converting Zebra Crossings to Signalised Crossings to Enhance Safety for Elderly Pedestrians
results.summary: Zebra crossing upgrades to signalised crossings are assessed holistically based on site-specific conditions including traffic patterns, road width, and the proportion of elderly residents. The Land Transport Authority prioritises busier environments and estates with higher senior populations, implementing Silver Zones and Friendly Streets that feature extended crossing times and narrowed road sections. The Authority aims to expand the Friendly Streets initiative to all Housing and Development Board towns by 2030.

results.title: Criteria for Immediate Mid-tenure Licence Shortening and Median Time Taken Between Investigation and Issuance of Corresponding Enforcement Actions in Past Three Years
results.summary: The Early Childhood Development Agency (ECDA) determines enforcement actions, including immediate mid-tenure licence shortening, by reviewing breach severity, systemic nature, and aggravating or mitigating factors. Penalties range from financial fines to licence shortening or revocation. The Minister noted that investigation and action timelines vary based on case complexity and nature. Specific median time data for the past three years was not provided in the response.

results.title: Data for Number of Calls to and Full-time Counsellors Manning National Mindline 1771
results.summary: Since launching in June 2025, National Mindline 1771 has received over 47,000 calls, messages, and webchats. As of December 2025, the dropped-calls rate was approximately 12%, though users can access immediate support via WhatsApp and webchat 24/7. The service is primarily staffed by full-time counsellors with plans to hire more to meet demand. Additionally, more than 25 volunteers have been trained and are available for deployment as needed.

results.title: Data of Rental Costs for Retail and F&B Sectors, and Firms Experiencing Rental Increases Upon Lease Renewal from 2019 to 2024
results.summary: Between 2019 and 2024, rental costs as a share of total business costs declined from 26% to 17% for the food and beverage services sector and from 30% to 26% for the retail trade sector. The Government does not hold data on the median or interquartile ranges of these rental cost shares, nor does it track the proportion of firms experiencing rental increases exceeding 10%, 20%, or 30% upon lease renewal during this period. Consequently, specific statistics regarding firms facing significant rental hikes upon renewal remain unavailable for public reference.

results.title: Data on Appeals from Car Buyers Affected by Recent PARF Rebate Cuts and Transitional Support Offered to Affected Buyers
results.summary: Fewer than 2,000 car buyers with committed purchases are estimated to be affected by immediate Preferential Additional Registration Fee (PARF) rebate cuts, with under 30 appeals received. The Ministry states these changes avoid distorting the Certificate of Entitlement market, following the same approach used in 2023. While the purchase remains a commercial arrangement between dealer and buyer, some dealers have reportedly made alternative arrangements for affected buyers. No transitional provision is currently proposed, and the Acting Minister for Transport did not confirm further support measures beyond existing dealer actions.

results.title: Data on Applications for and Approval Rates of Permanent Residency by Singapore Citizens for Aged Parents from 2020 to 2025
results.summary: From 2020 to 2025, the Immigration and Checkpoints Authority (ICA) processed an average of 1,900 annual applications for Permanent Residence by Singapore citizens for their aged parents, with an average approval rate of 1%. These figures reflect the strict eligibility constraints and low success rates for this specific category. The data highlights the limited availability of PR status for aged parents under current immigration policies.

results.title: Data on Cases of Wrongful Trading Investigated under Insolvency, Restructuring and Dissolution Act and Enforcement Outcomes in Past Three Years
results.summary: In the past three years, no suspected wrongful trading cases were reported to the Official Receiver under section 239(6) of the Insolvency, Restructuring and Dissolution Act 2018, as investigations fall under their purview rather than ACRA. Consequently, there were no prosecutions, civil actions, or other enforcement outcomes recorded for this specific offence during this period. While convictions can result in fines up to $10,000 or imprisonment for up to three years, civil claims for compensation remain independent of these investigations. The Ministry of Law does not maintain statistics on the number of civil actions pursued for wrongful trading in the last three years.

results.title: Data on Participation Rates for Future Care Planning Programmes in Past Five Years and Measures to Encourage Participation
results.summary: Between 2021 and 2025, 1,975 care plans were developed for families caring for persons with disabilities via SG Enable or social service agencies. SG Enable launched the Future Care Planning Resource Centre in December 2025 to enhance support access. The Ministry of Social and Family Development and SG Enable are strengthening sector capabilities to enable more caregivers to access future care planning support. Specific participation rates for the past five years were not provided in the response.

results.title: Data on Preschool Centre Closures without Six-months' Notice over Past Three Years and Enforcement Actions Taken against Operators
results.summary: Under the Early Childhood Development Centres Act, licensees must provide six months' notice before closing; however, non-compliance rose from 26 centres in 2023 to 44 in 2025. The Early Childhood Development Agency (ECDA) enforces this by issuing warning letters, financial penalties, or barring future licences based on case-specific facts and proportionality. Errant operators face these regulatory actions, with ECDA considering the specific circumstances of each breach before determining the necessary enforcement measures.

results.title: Data on Student Transfers between Mainstream and SPED Schools in Past 10 Years
results.summary: In 2023, the Ministry of Education introduced a central application process for transfers between mainstream and Special Education (SPED) schools. Since then, an average of 200 students annually transfer from mainstream to SPED schools, with 90% at the primary level and 70% diagnosed with Intellectual Disability. Conversely, 159 students transferred from SPED to mainstream schools, mostly at the secondary level, where 70% have Autism Spectrum Disorder and can access the national curriculum. Data prior to 2023 is unavailable as transfers were previously decentralised to schools.

results.title: Detection and Identification of Unsafe Children's Products Sold on E-commerce Platforms
results.summary: Under the Consumer Protection (Consumer Goods Safety Requirements) Regulations 2011, the Consumer Product Safety Office (CPSO) enforces international safety standards for children's products in Singapore. The agency conducts regular market surveillance by testing selected items for hazardous substances and collaborates with online and physical suppliers to identify and remove non-compliant products from e-commerce platforms. If harmful substances exceeding safety limits are detected, suppliers are directed to cease supply immediately. Non-compliance with these directions may result in fines or imprisonment.

results.title: Detection of Organised Criminal Activity in Recent Illegal Wildlife Trade Enforcement Operations
results.summary: The National Parks Board executed coordinated raids under Interpol's Operation Thunder in 2025, seizing 16 wildlife specimens. The Minister for National Development confirmed that no organised criminal activity was detected in Singapore during this operation or in other recent illegal wildlife trade enforcement efforts. Consequently, there are no reported implications for Singapore's ports, companies, or financial systems arising from organised criminal involvement in these specific operations.

results.title: Difference between Non-combat and Prohibited Involvement with Foreign Militaries
results.summary: On 24 March 2026, the Ministry of Home Affairs and Ministry of Defence issued a joint statement clarifying that two Singaporeans who volunteered with Sar-El in December 2016 were not involved in military activities with the Israel Defense Forces. Although their 2016 conduct required no action, the Government now deems involvement with Sar-El unacceptable given its current support for the IDF. The Internal Security Department advised the individuals that their 2025 blog post linking to the group was inappropriate; the individual subsequently removed it voluntarily. Authorities will consider action under the Internal Security Act against anyone who proceeds to volunteer with Sar-El or similar entities associated with foreign militaries, as such involvement is prejudicial to national security.

results.title: Effect of Deliveroo's Exit on Platform Work Economy, and Worker Earnings, Conditions and Welfare
results.summary: Following Deliveroo's exit, platform workers in Singapore retain access to 17 remaining operators. In 2025, the National Trades Union Congress, platform work associations, and operators jointly established industry-wide principles ensuring fair earnings and safe incentives. Workers with concerns regarding payment rates, benefits, or terms of service may directly raise them with their operators or associations, which will lead discussions based on these principles. The Ministry noted that workers can choose among the remaining operators, though specific impacts on bargaining power or overall earnings were not quantified in the response.

results.title: Effect of Rising Energy Prices on Cost of Singapore's Water Production
results.summary: Rising energy costs increase PUB's operating expenses for energy-intensive water production like desalination and NEWater, though fixed-rate electricity contracts moderate near-term impacts. The Ministry reviews water prices regularly to reflect supply costs while exploring technologies to reduce energy intensity. To maintain affordability, the Government provides U-Save rebates to eligible households to offset utility expenses. The Minister stated that these measures ensure water prices remain stable and affordable for both households and businesses.

results.title: Effectiveness of Cyclist Safety Measures Along Tanah Merah Coast Road and Further Infrastructure Improvements
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport reported that cyclist safety at Tanah Merah Coast Road is supported by coloured markings, raised bus stop islands, enhanced enforcement, and daily cleaning. These measures reduced overloading offences by 74% and illegal parking offences by 5% in the second half of 2025 compared to the first half. Regarding a physical separator, the Minister stated it would constrain cyclist manoeuvring space; instead, raised chevron markings alert motorists to lane encroachment. Cyclists avoiding road cycling can utilize the physically separated Park Connector Network running alongside the road.

results.title: Effectiveness of KidSTART Programme in Key Child and Family Outcomes
results.summary: KidSTART was rolled out nationwide in 2025, with the Ministry noting that more time is required to observe its impact. A prior evaluation study (2017–2022) found improved child development and pro-social behaviours by age three, findings presented at the 2024 Asian Family Conference that informed the decision to scale the revised service model. The Ministry will continue monitoring outcomes and assessing effectiveness following the nationwide rollout. No immediate review of the programme's reach has been prompted by the 2025 findings.

results.title: Effectiveness of Protected Non-teaching Time for TSNs on Student Interventions and Support in Mainstream Schools
results.summary: The Minister clarified that Teachers Trained in Special Needs (TSNs) do not conduct separate intervention sessions, as these are primarily delivered by Special Educational Needs (SEN) officers and school counsellors. Instead, TSNs adopt inclusive pedagogy within classrooms to support students with Special Educational Needs (SEN) and strengthen other educators' capacity. Schools calibrate TSN and SEN officer duties based on specific contexts and student needs, with higher-need schools receiving additional manpower resourcing. The Ministry has not mandated a minimum intervention-to-administration time ratio for TSNs, leaving workload calibration to individual school contexts.

results.title: Effectiveness of SwimSafer Programme in Developing Essential Swimming and Water Survival Skills for All Students
results.summary: The Minister stated that Primary schools typically offer SwimSafer 2.0 Stage 1 at the Primary 3 level, with higher levels available for proficient students. The Ministry does not set target percentages for proficiency achievement nor track student levels beyond the compulsory school programme. Students who fail to meet Stage 1 requirements may access a fully funded remediation programme provided by Sport Singapore to attain basic water survival skills. Additional learning is also available through the broader SwimSingapore framework outside the school curriculum.

results.title: Encouraging More Graduates to Enter Sea-based Farming Sector
results.summary: The Singapore Food Agency (SFA) does not track whether aquaculture graduates enter sea-based farms, prioritizing farm productivity metrics instead. However, SFA collaborates with local Institutes of Higher Learning (IHLs), including Temasek Polytechnic and Republic Polytechnic, to shape curricula and facilitate internships. Manpower projections for aquaculture training intake are calibrated against industry developments like new farm openings and recruitment plans. SFA provides regular inputs to the Ministry of Education and other agencies to inform adjustments to programme intakes based on these projected workforce needs.

results.title: Effectiveness of Tax Incentives Provided to Foreign Companies on Job Creation and Wage Enhancement in Past Five Years
results.summary: Tax incentives awarded to foreign companies represent a fraction of their projected total investment value, contingent on achieving specific economic outcomes including job creation and local employment. For instance, Economic Development Board commitments in 2025 are expected to generate 15,700 new jobs over the next five years, with approximately two-thirds offering gross monthly wages above $5,000, primarily to locals. The Government employs a multi-pronged strategy that combines incentives for anchor foreign investments with enterprise development grants supporting local companies to transform and grow. This approach aims to enhance productivity and competitiveness, enabling companies to create quality jobs and pay higher wages without a direct cost-per-job comparison against other schemes.

results.title: Enforcement Actions Taken for Failure to Display Preschool Licences Prominently and Publicly in Past Five Years
results.summary: Under the Early Childhood Development Centres Act, preschools must prominently display their licences, with tenure details accessible via LifeSG. The Early Childhood Development Agency reports no enforcement actions in the past five years as operators generally comply. Instances of missing or outdated licences were resolved immediately upon notification to the preschools.

results.title: Enforcement Actions Taken for Fraudulent GIRO Transactions
results.summary: The Police do not track the number of enforcement actions taken against individuals or company directors for fraudulent GIRO transactions in the past three years. This is because erroneous transactions may stem from administrative mistakes rather than criminal intent. Each reported case is assessed individually based on its specific facts and circumstances. If a criminal offence is established, the Police will investigate accordingly. No aggregate statistics or specific timelines for enforcement actions are available.

results.title: Enforcement Measures against Loud Vehicles and Adjusting Night-time Noise Thresholds for Residential Areas
results.summary: The Minister stated that Singapore's vehicle noise limits for new and in-use vehicles align with international standards and have been progressively tightened. She noted that setting separate day- and night-time thresholds is impractical as vehicle noise characteristics are independent of time. Since 2023, the National Environment Agency has increased enforcement coverage and frequency through joint blitzes at checkpoints and targeted road operations based on public feedback. The agency will continue monitoring feedback to assess potential standard adjustments or future enforcement actions while exploring new technologies to facilitate enforcement against noisy vehicles.

results.title: Enhanced Traffic Enforcements Against Speeding Motorcyclists on BKE and SLE Following Accidents Involving Malaysian Motorcyclists in 2025
results.summary: The Traffic Police has intensified enforcement against speeding motorcyclists at accident-prone stretches on the Bukit Timah Expressway (BKE) and Seletar Expressway (SLE), deploying resources based on violation patterns. In conjunction with the Land Transport Authority, targeted road calming measures, including speed-regulating strips along the BKE, have been introduced. The authorities will continue to review the necessity of additional measures. This response addresses concerns regarding accidents involving Malaysian motorcyclists in 2025 without specifying a new statutory increase in enforcement actions beyond the stated intensification.

results.title: Establishment of Nutritional Benchmarks for Migrant Workers' Catered Meals and Compliance Assessment Framework
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower will not establish nutritional benchmarks or dietary guidelines for catered meals provided to migrant workers, as only 13% of workers receive such meals while eight in ten cook or purchase their own. Instead, MOM focuses on educating workers through videos, posters, and roadshows in collaboration with primary healthcare anchor operators and non-governmental organisations. For food-related issues, workers can report concerns via the FWMOMCare mobile application or approach Forward Assurance and Support Team officers, prompting MOM to follow up with employers or dormitory operators to resolve the matter.

results.title: Extending SG Enable Training Course Fee Subsidies for Persons With Disabilities to SkillsFuture-eligible Programmes
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the Inter-Ministry Taskforce on Assurance for Families with Persons with Disabilities is currently studying ways to better support persons with disabilities in accessing training. This review could include extending the existing up to 95% course fee subsidies under the SG Enable Training Grant to SkillsFuture-eligible programmes offered by mainstream training providers. Specific details regarding the scope of these potential measures will be announced when ready.

results.title: Factors Contributing to Weaker Full-time Permanent Employment Outcomes in Selected Courses for 2025 Graduates
results.summary: The 2025 Graduate Employment Survey indicates a decline in full-time permanent employment rates, with the arts, design, and media cluster seeing the largest drop of 12 percentage points compared to 2024. However, overall employment within six months remains high at 88.9%, reflecting a return to pre-pandemic levels as post-pandemic hiring surges moderate. The Ministry attributes this shift to cautious corporate hiring amid economic uncertainties and evolving AI impacts, noting that flexible work arrangements are increasing the proportion of graduates in voluntary part-time roles. To address the gap, the Government launched the Graduate Industry Traineeships (GRIT) and GRIT@Gov Programmes in October 2025, which have been extended to the 2026 cohort to provide structured industry experience.

results.title: Factors Triggering Active Population Control of Otter Population and Measures to Safeguard Broader Ecosystem
results.summary: In 2024 and 2025, approximately half of the over 600 annual otter feedback cases received by NParks involved direct human-wildlife conflict. The National Parks Board employs sterilisation as a long-term population control measure, implemented in partnership with the Otter Working Group. Concurrent efforts include enhanced public education through advisory signage at identified conflict zones. NParks will continue monitoring the otter population and assessing the ecological impact of these management measures to safeguard the broader ecosystem.

results.title: Findings from Trial Deployment of Traffic Violation Enforcement Cameras
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for National Security reported that Traffic Violation Enforcement Cameras (TVECs), utilizing video analytics and automatic number plate recognition, detected over 67,000 violations including red-light running and illegal U-turns during the April 2025 to February 2026 trial. These mobile units operate round-the-clock to supplement manual enforcement. Following the trial, the Traffic Police deployed nine TVECs nationwide starting March 2026 and is developing plans to increase their numbers in the future. The system captures offences in real time that are currently enforced manually by officers.

results.title: GLCs' R&D Contributions and Activities under RIE2025/RIE2030 and Assessment of Singapore's National Innovation Performance
results.summary: BERD from local enterprises, including Government-linked Companies (GLCs), grew to $1.7 billion by 2023, representing approximately 20% of the total $9 billion national BERD. GLCs are actively undertaking R&D aligned with RIE2025/RIE2030 domains, such as decarbonisation, artificial intelligence, and advanced manufacturing. For instance, ST Engineering plans to expand its AI talent pool from 2,000 to 5,000 over five years via its new Artificial Intelligence Centre of Excellence. The Government notes that private sector R&D jobs grew by 36% between 2016 and 2023, reaching over 30,000 roles, with locals filling more than 70% of these positions. Singapore remains the sixth largest exporter of high-tech goods globally.

results.title: Grants or Subsidies to Support Local Law Firms in Adopting AI Tools
results.summary: The Minister for Law confirmed existing grant programmes support local law firms in adopting AI tools, including the Productivity Solutions Grant for the Legal Sector (PSG-Legal) which covers 50% of first-year costs for pre-approved legaltech solutions up to $45,000 annually. Supported categories include AI tools like legal chatbots and document review software, alongside the Enterprise Development Grant (EDG) and standard Productivity Solutions Grant (PSG) for broader productivity upgrades. To address change management challenges, particularly for smaller firms, the Ministry launched the Legal Innovation and Future-Readiness Transformation (LIFT) pilot in June 2025. Under this initiative, legaltech consultants are deployed to oversee end-to-end implementation, diagnose technology needs, and recommend suitable solutions, ensuring firms do not need to dedicate additional hours to research and implementation.

results.title: Gross Monthly Income Data of Full-time PMETs in Different Percentiles
results.summary: In 2025, the gross monthly income (excluding employer CPF) for full-time resident Professionals, Managers and Executives (PMEs) ranged from $4,500 at the 10th percentile to $13,900 at the 80th percentile, with a median of $8,400. The Ministry of Manpower stated it constantly monitors these wage levels alongside the operating environment to ensure existing employment laws and regulations remain fit for purpose and adequately represent PMEs. No specific timeline for legislative review or new eligibility criteria was provided, though the Minister confirmed ongoing assessment of these factors.

results.title: Help for Delivery Riders Affected by Recent Exit of Food Delivery Platform Company
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) does not disclose the specific number of delivery riders affected by a platform company's exit due to confidentiality requirements under the Statistics Act. Unlike employees, platform workers are not subject to employment termination notice periods or mandatory retrenchment notifications, as they retain the autonomy to choose their next gig. Consequently, standard employee support measures like statutory notice periods do not apply. Instead, affected riders can access career matching services, training programmes, and job coaching through Workforce Singapore (WSG), the Employment and Employability Institute (e2i), and relevant platform work associations. Additionally, workers from lower-income households may seek financial assistance from their Social Service Office regardless of their employment type.

results.title: Help for Employees Who Face Difficulty in Recovering Unpaid Salaries Due To Required Court Enforcement
results.summary: According to the Employment Standards Report 2024, 94% of employees recovering unpaid salaries via TADM achieved full recovery, while 4% received partial assistance; the remaining 2% who failed to recover were primarily higher income earners. Once a settlement is registered with the District Court, it becomes an enforceable order, allowing workers to seek TADM assistance for employer engagement or apply for Court enforcement if payment is missed. For serious non-payment cases, the Ministry of Manpower investigates potential Employment Act offences, imposing penalties including warnings, work pass debarment, and prosecution. Convicted employers face fines up to $15,000 and/or up to six months' imprisonment for first offences, or fines up to $30,000 and/or up to 12 months' imprisonment for repeat offenders.

results.title: Help for Singaporeans Staying or Travelling in Affected Areas amid Conflict Escalation in Middle East
results.summary: Following the March 2026 halt of commercial flights due to Middle East conflict escalation, the Singapore Government executed four repatriation flights and 13 overland bus journeys, returning 618 Singaporeans, Permanent Residents, and dependants from the UAE, Jordan, Bahrain, Qatar, and Kuwait. While spare seats were offered to 84 foreign nationals, approximately 2,000 eRegistered Singaporeans remain in the region under close contact with overseas Missions. With commercial flights now resumed, no additional repatriation flights are planned, and the existing MFA Travel Advisory remains valid. The Ministry urges all Singaporeans to defer travel to the region until further notice.

results.title: Energy Conservation (Amendment) Bill
results.summary: The Energy Conservation (Amendment) Bill 2026 closes a regulatory gap by extending Minimum Energy Performance Standards (MEPS) and Mandatory Energy Labelling Scheme (MELS) requirements to regulated goods imported by end users for own use. Under the new framework, importers must register goods with the National Environment Agency (NEA), ensure compliance with international test standards, and affix required energy labels. While the Bill aims to address uneven application and life-cycle costs from inefficient imports, stakeholders raised concerns regarding SME compliance costs, enforcement capacity for small-scale e-commerce shipments, and potential financial barriers for low-income households. The government noted that Clause 5 specifically targets direct imports, while Clause 7 grants the Director-General flexibility to modify conditions, though requests for transitional assistance, fee waivers, and expanded Energy Efficiency Grants remain under consideration to mitigate upfront costs and ensure social justice.

results.title: Impact of Industrial Activities and Developments in Loyang and Changi North Industrial Estates on Nearby Residents
results.summary: The Government coordinates planning across industrial and aviation sectors to minimise disamenities and environmental impacts, with JTC conducting Transport Impact Assessments for major developments alongside the Land Transport Authority. JTC has no plans to develop new industrial estates in Loyang and Changi North in the near term. The Government assures that all development entities, including JTC and LTA, holistically assess impacts on residents alongside business needs. Future planning will review buffer zones and land use strategies to ensure industrial expansion does not compromise resident liveability, with further details on congestion mitigation available in the Ministry of Transport's reply to oral Question No 35 at the 3 March 2026 Sitting.

results.title: Inclusion of Data of Households in Monthly Income Band of More Than $20,000
results.summary: The Department of Statistics Singapore (DOS) will expand the household market income bands beyond the current $20,000 ceiling in upcoming releases. This adjustment follows a recent review to ensure data relevance and aligns with the structure of the earlier household employment income series to facilitate cross-series comparison. DOS regularly evaluates these statistics to meet evolving user needs. The specific new band thresholds and exact release schedule for the expanded categories were not stated in the transcript.

results.title: Inclusion of Wealth Mobility Trend Analysis in Occasional Paper on Income Growth, Inequality and Social Mobility Trends
results.summary: The Ministry has published its first compilation of household wealth estimates derived from the 2023 Household Expenditure Survey. However, a study on wealth mobility trends is not currently planned because the necessary longitudinal data spanning multiple generations is unavailable. The Government will continue to enhance wealth data collection to enable future analysis. Consequently, the specific request to include wealth mobility trend analysis in the Occasional Paper cannot be fulfilled at this time due to these data constraints.

results.title: Impact of War in Iran on Singaporeans and Singapore's Businesses, Trade, Supply Chains, LNG Stockpiles and Prices
results.summary: MPs queried whether diesel and petrol price adjustments align with wholesale costs and if temporary or permanent pump price mechanisms are under consideration. They also sought projections on electricity price increases over the next 6–12 months and measures to shield households from spikes, alongside questions on adopting Australia's symmetrical cost-pass-through obligations following its 2025 review. The debate addressed the impact of the Iran conflict on supply chains, LNG stockpiles, and the cost of living, with specific focus on U-Save rebates, CDC vouchers, and temporary fuel excise duty reductions for transport-dependent businesses. Questions were raised regarding the adequacy of strategic food reserves, SFA stockpiling strategies, and support for sectors beyond oil and gas facing price or supply challenges.

results.title: Independent Audits and Verification of Algorithms Used in Third-party Predictive Analytics or Data-fusion Software Utilised by Government Security Agencies
results.summary: Government security agencies utilizing third-party predictive analytics or data-fusion software conduct independent audits and assessments both before and after deployment to ensure compliance with the Ministry of Home Affairs' AI governance framework. All such software, including underlying algorithms from external vendors, undergoes evaluation under this framework where risk assessment and mitigation measures are applied to manage operational risks. The Ministry confirms that these procedures verify the methodology of the software before it is relied upon for operational decisions, ensuring that independent verification is maintained throughout the application lifecycle.

results.title: Integrating Government Digital Systems to Ensure Effective Implementation of Court Orders for Sole Custody Care
results.summary: Agencies will consider Family Justice Court orders regarding sole custody, care, and control of children when administering relevant policies or services. The specific implementation of these orders within Government digital systems across Ministries and Statutory Boards depends on each agency's unique context and requirements. No mandatory timeline or uniform operational standard for digital system recognition was stated. The Minister clarified that recognition is discretionary based on policy relevance rather than a blanket requirement for all digital platforms.

results.title: Keeping Public Transport Fares Affordable Beyond Vouchers and Concession Passes for Commuters
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that the Public Transport Council (PTC) already incorporates household income affordability metrics into annual fare adjustments, utilizing discretion to defer increases when necessary. Data indicates the proportion of household income spent on transport fell from 3.1% in 2015 to 2.4% in 2024 for lower-income households. Current support includes concession passes offering up to 55% discounts, monthly expenditure caps, and Public Transport Vouchers covering roughly half a year of fare increases for typical families. The Government provides over $2 billion in annual subsidies, with an additional $200 million allocated for the 2025 Fare Review to cover deferred increases. The Minister stated that introducing a binding affordability cap unrelated to operational costs could cause fares to diverge from costs, requiring further taxpayer subsidies.

results.title: Legal Disclosure Frameworks for Lawyers to Access Police Investigations for Civil Proceedings
results.summary: The Police may provide investigation information or documents to persons directly involved in the investigations or their legal representatives for use in civil proceedings upon application. Each request is assessed on a case-by-case basis, with consultation involving the Attorney-General's Chambers where necessary. The Minister did not confirm a formal framework exists but indicated that applications are currently handled individually rather than through a standardized disclosure mechanism. No specific safeguards like redaction or Court oversight were explicitly mandated as standard procedure in the stated response.

results.title: Local Employee Headcount, Salary and Seniority Distribution Measurements for Foreign Companies Receiving Tax Incentives
results.summary: Foreign companies receiving tax incentives must achieve specified economic outcomes, including local job creation, with targets calibrated to each project's nature and scope rather than fixed annual percentage increases. The Government maintains mechanisms to revoke incentives and recover benefits from companies failing to meet commitments, though the vast majority successfully fulfill their obligations. Specific penalties, claw-back provisions, the number of terminated incentives in the past five years, and exact monetary recovery amounts were not stated in the response. Eligibility for incentives is granted judiciously only when economic agencies assess that they are necessary to secure investment and generate net economic benefits.

results.title: Local Industries Affected by Increase in US Tariffs and Measures to Help Affected SMEs
results.summary: The US Supreme Court struck down reciprocal tariffs on 20 February 2026, replacing them with a 10% Section 122 tariff effective 24 February 2026, with a potential increase to 15% pending official directive. Industries with high US dependence, specifically precision engineering and parts of general manufacturing, face greater exposure to any rate hike. The Singapore Economic Resilience Taskforce (SERT) is gathering feedback to guide support measures. The Business Adaptation Grant (BizAdapt) assists firms in evaluating impacts and optimizing supply chains, with support levels raised from 50% to 70% at Budget 2026. Additionally, the Graduate Industry Traineeship scheme was launched to help fresh graduates gain skills amid economic uncertainty. The Government remains committed to providing further assistance as needed.

results.title: Local Recruitment Patterns for Scam Money Mule Networks and Measures to Disrupt Recruiter Pipelines
results.summary: Police cannot confirm the specific breakdown of the 167 individuals under investigation as inquiries continue, noting that evidence often fails to prove syndicate membership. However, observations indicate most money mules facilitate syndicate activities, which recruit primarily via online messaging and social media. To disrupt these pipelines, the Police issue directions under the Online Criminal Harms Act (OCHA) to remove scam-related accounts and advertisements. Additionally, online platforms must comply with ex ante requirements under the OCHA Codes of Practice to reduce scam prevalence. Regular public education campaigns by the Police and National Crime Prevention Council also raise awareness of recruitment tactics and associated penalties.

results.title: Medical Consultations due to Haze-induced Symptoms
results.summary: To date this year, the Ministry reports no significant increase in attendances for haze-relevant conditions at public hospital Emergency Departments and polyclinics. While seniors remain a vulnerable group prone to respiratory exacerbations due to age and existing conditions, the Ministry monitors these risks closely. The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health highlighted that Community Health Posts serve as "always on" platforms within Active Ageing Centres to provide immediate access to seniors. These posts facilitate regular engagements, including telemedicine, medical service delivery, and medication support, ensuring they function as critical touchpoints during sudden crises or haze events.

results.title: Medical Records Access Rights for Non-custodial Parents and Court-ordered Custody Protections
results.summary: Non-custodial parents can access but not modify their child's medical records on HealthHub, as records are managed exclusively by healthcare professionals. Requests for access under special circumstances, such as sole custody arrangements, must be submitted via email to contact_us@healthhub.sg. The Ministry of Health evaluates these requests individually without a standardized safeguard framework currently in place. No automatic access rights or specific legal protections for non-custodial parents were confirmed in the response.

results.title: Obligations on Licensed Telecommunications Operators Under Current Service and Resiliency Frameworks after Major Unplanned Disruption
results.summary: Between 16 and 18 March 2026, Singtel experienced three service incidents affecting over 600,000 mobile customers, with the primary disruption lasting nine hours due to a mechanical fault. The Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA) is investigating these events, noting no evidence of cyber-attacks or impact on emergency services, while Singtel has issued one-time rebates to affected subscribers. Licensed operators must resolve incidents within one hour under the Telecommunications Act and Telecom Service Resiliency Code, facing enforcement actions or financial penalties for non-compliance. Providers are also required to maintain over 99% nationwide outdoor mobile coverage, conduct regular infrastructure audits, and implement redundancies for core network functions. IMDA will publish investigation findings upon completion and may update regulations based on industry suggestions and identified lapses.

results.title: Outcomes and Effectiveness of AI-related SkillsFuture Programmes
results.summary: The Ministry of Education and SkillsFuture Singapore (SSG) track placement rates for AI-related SkillsFuture Career Transition Programmes (SCTPs) but do not track median wage changes, citing multiple influencing factors. Among 8,000 ICT-related SCTP completers between June 2022 and March 2025, 44% secured new roles or employment within six months as of 30 September 2025. Placement outcomes for shorter courses are generally untracked, as these programmes aim to equip learners with current skills to enhance performance in existing roles. Beyond placement data, SSG publishes Training Quality and Outcomes Measurement (TRAQOM) survey results on the MySkillsFuture portal, including feedback on whether training improved job effectiveness and allowed for enhanced responsibilities.

results.title: Outcomes of Archival Documentation Declassification Requests in Past 10 Years
results.summary: Between 2016 and 2025, Government agencies reviewed over 9,000 records in response to 4,491 public requests, with 72% approved for access. The National Archives of Singapore (NAS) has declassified more than 74,000 records, while recent releases include the Albatross File and Mr Lee Kuan Yew's Oral History scheduled for next year. Rejections primarily stem from national security, confidentiality obligations, and personal privacy concerns. The Minister noted that the Government may proactively release documents to ensure a complete historical record, such as key milestones. Specific annual breakdowns of requests and the top five rejection reasons for each of the last 10 years were not provided in the response.

results.title: Outcomes of Pupils Placed in Learning Support Programme for English in Primary 1 and 2
results.summary: The Ministry tracks outcomes for pupils in the Learning Support Programme for English (Primary 1–2), noting that monitored progress narrows learning gaps. Students requiring continued support may join the Reading Remediation Programme in Primary 3–4 or the School-based Dyslexia Remediation Programme if diagnosed with dyslexia. Pupils advancing to Primary 5 can take standard English with optional RRP support or opt for the Foundation English curriculum. The Minister stated these targeted literacy strategies enable students to access the curriculum according to their abilities and progress.

results.title: Penalties for Companies that Contravene Mandatory Retrenchment Notification Requirement
results.summary: The Minister stated that raising penalties for Mandatory Retrenchment Notification (MRN) contraventions is not currently necessary due to high compliance rates. Under the Employment Act 1968, companies with 10+ employees must submit MRNs within five working days; the current penalty is $1,000 for the first offence and $2,000 for subsequent ones. In 2025, 81% of submissions met the deadline, up from 67% in 2024, with late submissions often attributed to administrative oversight. While caution letters are issued for delays, administrative penalties will be imposed on recalcitrant employers, and the enforcement framework will be reviewed periodically.

results.title: Pre-emptive Adjustments in Monetary Policy to Curb Energy Cost-driven Inflation
results.summary: The Monetary Authority of Singapore is currently assessing its monetary policy stance to address inflation driven by higher energy costs. The upcoming Monetary Policy Statement, scheduled for release on 14 April 2026, will include an updated inflation outlook for 2026. No specific pre-emptive adjustments have been confirmed at this time, as the MAS continues its evaluation process.

results.title: Preventing Identities of Children Involved in Child Protection Cases from Being Publicised or Disclosed
results.summary: Under the Children and Young Persons Act, Section 111(1) prohibits any person, including parents, from publishing information that identifies a child involved in a child protection investigation. The Ministry of Social and Family Development first advises parents to remove such content; if they refuse, the Ministry notifies platforms, applies to the Court for removal under Section 111(2), or refers the matter to the Police. The Ministry notes that parents facing family crises may not fully appreciate the risks of online disclosure, which can compromise child safety and undermine professional interventions. Consequently, the Ministry will continue strengthening public education and encouraging parents to raise concerns through confidential channels to prioritize the child's interests.

results.title: Procedures for Contacting Next-of-kin and Management of Unaccompanied Accident and Emergency Patients
results.summary: There is no fixed average timeline for contacting next-of-kin in public hospitals, as procedures depend on the specific clinical situation and urgency of care. In Accident and Emergency cases involving unaccompanied patients, clinicians prioritize life-saving interventions and contact next-of-kin as soon as possible. For non-communicative patients, hospitals establish identity and contact details through records or, as a last resort, collaborate with the police. The Minister noted that standard operating procedures are situation-dependent rather than strictly defined by a set duration.

results.title: Product Design Requirements for Trading Card Box Designs to Mitigate Gambling Risks
results.summary: The Ministry of Home Affairs plans to release blind box regulations around mid-2026. While additional safeguards like probability disclosure and minimum guaranteed rarity distributions may be considered subsequently, they will only be implemented if deemed proportionate to the gambling inducement risk. The Ministry requires further time to study these measures before finalizing the regulatory framework. Specifics on how these standards compare to disclosure-based measures are not yet provided.

results.title: Proportion of Repeat Bird-Feeding Offenders Issued with Fines in Past Three Years
results.summary: In the past three years, approximately 30% of illegal bird-feeding cases involved repeat offenders. The Minister for National Development stated that all identified repeat offenders were issued fines, with the sole exception of one case currently pending Court proceedings. No specific percentage was provided for the proportion of repeat offenders who received fines, as the single pending case represents a minor deviation from the general enforcement pattern.

results.title: Proportion of Singapore's Inflation over Past Five Years Attributable to External Factors
results.summary: Energy and food components account for approximately 24% of the Consumer Price Index (CPI)-All Items basket, directly reflecting higher global commodity prices. From 2020 to 2025, CPI-All Items rose by 3.3% per annum, with these external factors contributing roughly 0.9 percentage-point to the increase. The Minister noted that imported inflation plays a significant role in Singapore's overall inflation outcomes due to the economy's heavy reliance on imports for both economic activity and domestic consumption. Consequently, global energy and food price fluctuations substantially influence domestic inflation metrics.

results.title: Proposal for Childcare Subsidies To Be Raised to Match Infant Care Subsidy Levels
results.summary: The Minister explained that infant care fees remain higher than childcare due to stricter staff-to-child ratios and specialized facility requirements, necessitating larger subsidies to maintain affordability. In 2025, median infant care fees were $1,235 versus $680 for childcare, resulting in full-day childcare costs of 3.1% of income for median-income families compared to 5.8% for infant care. Starting 2026, full-day childcare fees at anchor operator preschools will drop to a maximum of $610, with partner operators capped at $650. Further enhancements take effect on 1 January 2027, when the income ceiling for preschool subsidies rises from $12,000 to $15,000 and all subsidy tier thresholds will increase.

results.title: Proposal for Partial Home-based Learning to Reduce Transport Use amid Petrol Price Hikes
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated there are no plans to implement additional partial home-based learning arrangements despite rising petrol prices linked to the Middle East situation. Secondary schools and pre-university institutions currently conduct home-based learning approximately once every two weeks, while primary schools do not offer regular home-based learning. The Ministry will continue monitoring the broader economic situation and its impact on families but will not adjust existing home-based learning schedules at this time.

results.title: Protection against Unfair Business Practices for Small Retail Subtenants in Commercial Properties
results.summary: Since February 2024, the Lease Agreements for Retail Premises Act mandates compliance with the Fair Tenancy Industry Committee's Code of Conduct for qualifying retail leases. For small retail commercial properties up to 5,000 square feet with lease terms of three years or less, security deposits are capped at three months' gross rent. Disputes regarding lease negotiations or terms can be resolved through low-cost mediation or adjudication at the Singapore Mediation Centre. The Code aims to balance tenant cash flow protection with landlord reinstatement costs, and the Fair Tenancy Industry Committee is currently reviewing the Code, with completion expected later this year.

results.title: Provision of Express Bus Services to Areas Underserved by MRT Lines while Assessing Implementation of Proposed Seletar Line
results.summary: Under the Bus Connectivity Enhancement Programme, the Land Transport Authority (LTA) introduced MRT Direct Service 21X to Novena MRT station on 28 April 2025 for Whampoa residents, later renumbered Service 456 on 24 November 2025. The LTA will continue reviewing needs in Whampoa while considering requests elsewhere and overall resource constraints. The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed these improvements address areas underserved by MRT lines while the proposed Seletar Line implementation is studied.

results.title: Quantum Research Coordination Between A*STAR and NUS and Combined Annual Funding
results.summary: Under the National Quantum Strategy, the Centre for Quantum Technologies (CQT) at NUS is designated as the national flagship centre to coordinate research talent and support investigator-led initiatives across a network of university and A*STAR nodes. A*STAR's Quantum Innovation Centre (Q.InC) develops next-generation technologies in strategic pillars like quantum sensing and materials, with researchers jointly appointed to CQT and research agendas coordinated by the CQT Director to ensure alignment and synergy. In Research, Innovation and Enterprise 2025, $295 million was allocated to fund these quantum research efforts, overseen by the National Quantum Steering Committee. The transcript does not specify the exact combined annual funding beyond this allocation or detail the specific mechanisms preventing duplication beyond the stated coordination structures.

results.title: Impact of the Middle East Situation on Singapore
results.summary: The Government acknowledges that Singapore cannot fully insulate itself from the ongoing Middle East conflict, which has already impacted energy supplies and household costs, necessitating a coordinated, multi-agency response led by the Homefront Crisis Management Committee (HCMC) and Homefront Crisis Executive Group (HCEG). While rejecting broad fuel subsidy reductions as too blunt, the administration will supplement Budget 2026 measures with targeted support for affected transport workers and households, alongside efforts to secure diversified energy sources including potential LNG increases from Indonesia and Malaysia. Officials confirmed there are no immediate risks to jet fuel or seafood supplies, though secondary inflationary pressures remain possible; consequently, the Government will monitor prices closely and implement further assistance without disclosing specific fuel reserve or food stockpile details to avoid compromising crisis resilience.

results.title: Quantum of Expenditure on Government Contracts with Palantir Technologies or Subsidiaries
results.summary: Over the past five years, the Government procured $250,000 in software services from Palantir Technologies Singapore Pte Ltd, a subsidiary of Palantir Technologies Inc. This expenditure covers procurement contracts, software licences, or consultancy services across all Ministries, Statutory Boards, and Organs of State. The Minister for Finance confirmed this specific quantum without detailing the breakdown by agency or specific project types. No further information regarding the nature of these services or future procurement plans was provided in the response.

results.title: Reasons for Low Uptake by Couples for Genetic Screening
results.summary: The PREDICT programme, a pilot for recessive genetic disorder screening, screened only 674 couples by end-2025 against a 40,000 target for 2024–2027 due to low uptake. Key barriers include limited contact with preconception couples (mostly engaging pregnant or fertility/IVF patients), insurance concerns regarding positive results, and the complexity of testing for over 80 diseases. The Ministry of Health plans to review the pilot with KK Women's and Children's Hospital and Temasek Foundation.
