Proposed improvements involve addressing insurance underwriting via a new Act, adopting a disease-specific approach similar to familial hypercholesterolemia and BRCA protocols, and potentially expanding touchpoints to marriage registries. The Ministry will study implementation strategies to enhance the programme's potential.

results.title: Recognition of In-prison Employment History in HDB Housing Applications for Former Inmates and Data on Ex-offenders Denied HDB Grants in Last Three Years
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that HDB assesses grant and loan eligibility based on formal employment relationships to verify a household's long-term ability to service loans. Consequently, work programmes completed in prison are not considered valid employment history, and remuneration from these programmes cannot be included in HDB loan eligibility assessments or the fulfillment of the continuous employment condition for the Enhanced CPF Housing Grant. The Minister further clarified that the primary purpose of prison work programmes is offender rehabilitation rather than financial remuneration. Additionally, HDB does not maintain data on the number of ex-offenders denied housing grants due to failing the continuous employment condition, as applicants are not required to declare prior offence records during the flat application process.

results.title: Recognition for Contributions of SAF Personnel Involved in Recent Repatriation Flights from Middle East
results.summary: The Ministry of Defence will formally recognise SAF personnel involved in recent Middle East repatriation flights and develop public education initiatives to highlight the mission's complexity and courage. The SAF will profile the mission planning, execution, and background training within operational security bounds, while debriefing teams to share relevant lessons with servicemen, including non-public details. Regarding public sharing, the Ministry will highlight behind-the-scenes efforts through media channels as previously done. Additionally, the Ministry will consider including lessons from these successful multi-faceted rescue and evacuation operations in the syllabus for National Service commanders at the Officer Cadet School's Air Wing or Specialist Command School. This initiative aims to educate NSmen and soldiers on the collective efforts of SAF units, MFA, MINDEF, and other agencies that enabled the safe execution of the flights.

results.title: Redeployment of Children Affected by Centre Closures to Nearby Centres and Proportion of Unresolved Cases
results.summary: The Early Childhood Development Agency assists parents in securing alternative preschool placements when centres cease operations, including those with short notice. The vast majority of affected children secured new arrangements before their original centre's last day. While the Minister noted some parents delay enrolment due to personal preferences or waiting for specific vacancies, the transcript does not provide specific statistics on average redeployment timelines or the proportion of unresolved cases.

results.title: Retention Rate of Permanent Residents in Global Investor Programme Over Past 10 Years
results.summary: From 2016 to 2025, approximately 85% of Global Investor Programme (GIP) Permanent Residents retained their status as of 31 December 2025. Fewer than 25 individuals failed to renew re-entry permits due to unmet business milestones, while fewer than 20 were subsequently granted Singapore citizenship. The Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Trade and Industry provided these figures, confirming that retention is contingent on economic contribution and residency length. No further details regarding specific investment thresholds or programme changes were disclosed in the response.

results.title: Review of Implementation Timeline for and Quantum of Sustainable Aviation Fuel Levy Amid Rising Fuel Prices
results.summary: The Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore deferred the sustainable aviation fuel levy due to the Middle East conflict's impact on airlines and passengers. The levy now applies to tickets sold from 1 October 2026 for flights departing from 1 January 2027. The levy quantum remains unchanged from the November 2025 announcement, utilizing a fixed-cost envelope model to stabilize costs for air transport users. No review of the timeline or quantum was conducted despite rising fuel prices.

results.title: Retention of Face-to-face Services at Neighbourhood Police Centres and Inclusive Accessibility of Online Police Services for Elderly and Non-English Speakers
results.summary: Decisions on deploying unmanned Neighbourhood Police Centres (NPCs) or Neighbourhood Police Posts (NPPs) consider operational needs and infrastructure plans to maintain crime-fighting focus without reducing presence. While tracking online service accessibility for the elderly and non-English speakers is challenging due to difficulty distinguishing assisted from unassisted access, the Police engage these communities through multiple channels. Over 70% of police reports are lodged via self-help options, yet face-to-face assistance remains available at 33 NPCs and 64 NPPs islandwide. Additionally, visitors to unmanned facilities can use videocalls to speak with officers for guidance or remote report lodging.

results.title: Review of Nutri-Grade Framework to Appropriately Reflect Nutritional Value of Plain Milk and Distinguish Natural Dairy Fats from Unhealthy Additives
results.summary: The Nutri-Grade framework rates beverages by sugar and saturated fat levels, encouraging A and B ratings while discouraging C and D. For young children aged one to two, full-fat plain milk remains served in preschools per dietary guidelines, whereas older children are encouraged to consume reduced-fat milk that is Nutri-Grade A or B or meets Healthier Choice Symbol standards. Beverages with unhealthy additives, such as non-nutritive sweeteners, are excluded from schools regardless of their Nutri-Grade rating to prevent conditioning palates toward sweet foods. The Ministry of Health and Health Promotion Board acknowledged public feedback regarding full-fat milk availability and underweight students' preferences, committing to continue reviewing nutrition policies to ensure they remain relevant and evidence-based.

results.title: Review of Requirements for Window Tints in Motor Vehicles while Accounting for Heat Reduction, Visibility and Road Safety
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority allows vehicle owners to use tinted films with heat-reduction technologies, provided they meet specific visibility and safety criteria. Front windscreen and side windows must transmit at least 70% of visible light, while rear windows require a minimum of 25%. Films must also be non-reflective and not interfere with Electronic Road Pricing signal transmission. The Authority will continue reviewing these requirements with the Singapore Police Force to ensure they remain fit for purpose amidst changing technologies and weather conditions.

results.title: Reviewing Implementation of Increase in ERP Rates Amid Rising Petrol and Fuel Costs
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority increased ERP rates at four gantries in March 2026 to address persistently low speeds on the Ayer Rajah, Central, and Kallang-Paya Lebar Expressways. This adjustment aims to encourage motorists to travel at different times or use alternative routes to relieve congestion, as the primary objective of ERP is traffic management rather than revenue generation. The LTA reviews rates quarterly based on traffic conditions, and the recent increase was implemented despite rising fuel costs to maintain optimal road speeds.

results.title: Reviewing Requirement for Advance Medical Directives to be Signed in Doctor's Presence
results.summary: As of February 2026, over 84,500 Advance Medical Directives (AMDs) are registered, with the Ministry reviewing the process to increase uptake and streamline procedures. The Ministry maintains the requirement for a witnessing doctor to ensure clinical understanding and mental capacity, citing confidentiality safeguards that prevent inappropriate triggering of directives before a patient is certified terminally ill. While operational processes ensure prompt AMD search responses even outside office hours, the Ministry is conscious of specific circumstances and will consider feedback to further improve the process. The Minister noted that doctors can discuss AMD concepts and advanced care planning with conscious patients under existing Act exceptions, though inquiries about existing directives remain restricted to protect confidentiality.

results.title: Reviewing Shingles Vaccination Subsidy Frameworks Across Polyclinics and Private GP Clinics for Merdeka Generation Cardholders
results.summary: The Ministry clarified that achieving consistent out-of-pocket costs for shingles vaccination subsidies across polyclinics and private GP clinics is challenging due to differing Community Health Assist Scheme (CHAS) frameworks. At public institutions, subsidies for Merdeka Generation cardholders are means-tested, whereas CHAS GP clinics provide a fixed dollar subsidy to all eligible cardholders regardless of income. Consequently, cost variations between settings reflect these distinct subsidy structures rather than a lack of government support. No review of the subsidy frameworks was announced in response to feedback regarding higher charges at polyclinics.

results.title: Road Accidents Due To Vehicles Striking Pedestrians at Zebra Crossings and Turns
results.summary: The Traffic Police does not track accidents specifically involving vehicles turning into minor roads and striking pedestrians at zebra crossings. The Land Transport Authority states that junctions follow international safety standards, utilizing Red-Amber-Green arrows at most signalised intersections and single right-turn lanes where feasible. For remaining junctions, the Authority will consider adding prominent turning pockets, signage, and traffic calming measures like centre dividers or road humps to enhance safety and smooth traffic flow.

results.title: Safeguards to Ensure Citizen Data Is Not Disclosed to or Processed by Foreign-headquartered Vendors
results.summary: The Government employs a risk-based approach with technical safeguards, including data residency requirements, encryption, and non-retention policies, to protect citizen data when using foreign-headquartered vendors. These measures are coupled with governance frameworks and contractual agreements to prevent unauthorized access or disclosure, even under foreign legal compulsion. The Minister acknowledged that extraterritorial laws, such as the US CLOUD Act, can override contractual obligations, necessitating reliance on technical controls and governance to limit data use cases. Consequently, the Government continuously monitors vendor compliance and updates frameworks to address emerging risks, though specific details on contracts with vendors like Palantir were deferred to the Ministry of Finance.

results.title: Salary Arrangements for Political Office Holders Seconded to NTUC in Last 10 Years
results.summary: Political office holders (POHs) seconded to the National Trades Union Congress (NTUC) operate under a cost-sharing arrangement where the Government generally funds approximately 50% of the fixed salary component, including basic and 13th-month payments. This mutual funding model ensures both the Government and NTUC share responsibility for secondment outcomes while reinforcing their partnership in shaping worker-focused policies. All seconded POHs receive a single pay package consistent with the 2012 White Paper standards, and annual manpower expenditure budgets for each Ministry are published in the Budget book. Specific historical data on total dollar amounts or average wage percentages for the last decade were not provided in the response.

results.title: School-based Support and Intervention Strategies for Students with Chronic Absenteeism and Academic Decline Linked to Excessive Gaming
results.summary: The Ministry of Education identifies students with gaming concerns through Cyber Wellness lessons, teacher referrals, peer reports, and termly well-being check-ins. Schools intervene via counsellors and welfare officers to address underlying issues causing chronic absenteeism or academic decline linked to excessive gaming. Parents are engaged to monitor online activities, while additional cases are referred to Social Service Agencies or the National Addiction Management Service under the Institute of Mental Health for specialised treatment. Community partners like TOUCH Community Services also provide counselling programmes. The approach emphasises maintaining a healthy balance between online and offline activities to manage time spent on gaming.

results.title: Schools Tackling Impact of Posting Group Banding to Promote Inclusivity and Friendships
results.summary: Under Full Subject-Based Banding (Full SBB), secondary students attend mixed form classes where approximately one-third of curriculum time is spent on common subjects like Art and Character and Citizenship Education with peers from diverse subject levels. This structure aims to foster inclusivity and friendships by enabling interaction across different strengths and interests, complemented by co-curricular activities and Values-in-Action projects. To support emotional well-being in this diverse setting, the Ministry of Education provides teachers with professional development, termly check-in surveys to monitor student interests and peer support networks, and tools to map peer relationships. The Ministry also observes and shares best practices developed by teachers to equip the profession with strategies for managing unique mixed form classes effectively.

results.title: Secondment of Singaporean Public Officers to ASEAN Secretariat in Past Five Years and Plans to Increase Secondments Before, During and After Chairmanship
results.summary: Over the past five years, one Singaporean public officer, Mr Satvinder Singh, was seconded to the ASEAN Secretariat as Deputy Secretary-General for the ASEAN Economic Community via open recruitment. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs is exploring additional secondment opportunities for officers from various ministries to the Secretariat in the lead-up to, during, and after Singapore's 2027 ASEAN Chairmanship. These initiatives aim to broaden public service experience in international diplomacy and ASEAN tradecraft. While most Secretariat positions are filled through open recruitment, leadership roles like the Secretary-General and Deputy Secretaries-General are appointed by Member States or recruited based on merit. Singapore will appoint the Deputy Secretary-General for the ASEAN Socio-Cultural Community in 2027.

results.title: Selection Criteria for National Celebrations at Heartland Sites and Framework for Systemic Rotation of Celebrations Across Towns
results.summary: The People's Association (PA) selects heartland celebration sites based on operational constraints including site capacity, cost-effectiveness, accessibility, and terrain suitability for mobile columns, rather than a rigid multi-year rotational framework. For the 2025 National Day Heartland Celebrations, specific requirements included appropriate terrain and roads capable of closure for deployment. Consequently, the Government does not employ a fixed rotation system to ensure even spread across all towns; instead, site selection adapts annually to local needs and operational contexts. Generally, one celebration is held per district to ensure convenient resident access.

results.title: Singapore Prison Service Inmate's Daily Allowance for Full-time Work and Post-release Housing Affordability
results.summary: The Minister clarified that Singapore Prison Service inmate daily allowances are intentionally austere to uphold punishment and deterrence, with no correlation to asset accumulation like HDB downpayments. Work programmes prioritize rehabilitation, skill acquisition, and employability over financial remuneration, as earnings do not offset the high costs of housing, training, and rehabilitation programmes. Upon release, the Yellow Ribbon Singapore (YRSG) agency provides job matching, career coaching, and post-release support for up to 12 months to assist inmates in securing suitable employment. Consequently, the allowance system is not designed to facilitate savings for housing, and the primary objective remains preventing relapse by instilling positive work ethics rather than generating income.

results.title: Singapore's Comprehensive Response to US Section 301 Investigations on Overcapacity and Forced Labour
results.summary: The Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Trade and Industry directed that questions regarding Singapore's representation at US Section 301 public hearings on overcapacity and forced labour, as well as guidance for companies facing a 15 April 2026 submission deadline, be addressed via written replies to Questions No 64 to 69. These written responses were scheduled for the 7 April 2026 Parliament Sitting. The Minister did not provide immediate details on specific steps to demonstrate the adequacy of Singapore's legal frameworks prohibiting the importation of goods produced with forced labour during this oral exchange.

results.title: Strengthening Return to Work Pathways
results.summary: MP Melvin Yong urged the Government to strengthen return-to-work pathways by intervening earlier, equipping SMEs, and creating a dedicated pathway for workers unable to return to previous roles due to injury or illness. He proposed a time-bound wage support scheme for non-work-related conditions and MediSave usage for return-to-work assessments. Minister Dinesh Vasu Dash acknowledged these suggestions, noting that MOM is reviewing the landscape and will launch the Alliance for Action on Safety and Health for Employment Longevity (AfA-SHEL) in the second half of 2026 to crowdsource innovative solutions. The Ministry highlighted existing pilots, including the 2017–2021 hospital programme with 95% return-to-work success and the 2023–2025 Bounce Back Lab, while confirming that return-to-work expenses for work injuries remain claimable under the Work Injury Compensation Act.

results.title: Study of Affordability Levels in Punggol for Autonomous Vehicle Trial Pricing
results.summary: The $4 starting fare for Punggol autonomous vehicle trials was set by operators in consultation with the Ministry of Transport and Land Transport Authority. The Minister stated that fares are expected to adjust over time based on ridership demand. The response did not specify how the $4 fare was calculated, whether affordability levels for the Punggol population were studied, or what percentage of target ridership would find the fare affordable.

results.title: Take-up Rate of Enhanced Shared Parental Leave
results.summary: The take-up rate for the new 10-week Shared Parental Leave scheme, introduced on 1 April 2025, is not yet available as claims data is still being collected. Employers have an additional three months after the one-year utilisation period to submit claims, with MSF expected to release the 2025 birth cohort update in mid-2027. While definitive statistics on new fathers' participation are unavailable, the Government aims to encourage active fatherhood through partnerships like the Centre for Fathering and public outreach. Broader efforts to shift workplace norms and balance family needs are being examined by a work group chaired by Minister Indranee, though specific policy changes beyond the current scheme have not been detailed.

results.title: Temporary Targeted Support for Lower-income Ride-hail Drivers and Delivery Riders Without Access to Subsidised or Discounted Fuel
results.summary: The Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Trade and Industry directed that the question regarding temporary fuel rebates for lower-income ride-hail drivers and delivery riders be addressed in the Ministerial Statements on the Impact of the Middle East Situation on Singapore delivered on 7 April 2026. These statements, issued by the Acting Minister for Transport and Senior Minister of State for Finance, cover the government's response to rising fuel prices linked to the Middle East conflict. Specific details on whether time-limited fuel rebates or cash offsets will be implemented for drivers without access to subsidised fuel were not provided in this transcript.

results.title: Time Allocation Between Active Training and Waiting During In-camp Training for Reservists and Measures to Improve Time Management and Training Effectiveness
results.summary: The Singapore Armed Forces (SAF) ensures adequate rest and minimizes time imposition on National Service men (NSmen) by actively monitoring experiences via post-ICT surveys and acting on feedback. Key efficiency measures include decoupling the Individual Physical Proficiency Test (IPPT) from in-camp training to allow flexible completion, utilizing the OneNS app for digital administrative functions, and reducing waiting times through better pre-planning and decoupling small group from commanders' training. Facilities like the Multi-Mission Range Complex have been enhanced to support more efficient training conduct. The SAF continues to monitor and improve operational, training, and administrative efficiencies while ensuring safe activity conduct and sufficient recuperation for NSmen.

results.title: Timeline for Ongoing Review of Possible Ban on Flavoured Tobacco Products
results.summary: The Ministry is conducting an ongoing review of a possible ban on flavoured tobacco products, including menthol and fruit varieties, with no specific timeline provided. Findings from this review will influence the decision to institute a ban, alongside other suggested options like a nicotine-free generation policy. The Government currently prioritizes existing measures such as public education, taxes, and advertising bans, which have reduced smoking prevalence to 8.4%. Further details on the review's progress or specific criteria for a ban were not stated in the response.

results.title: Tracking Career Progression Outcomes for Persons with Disabilities
results.summary: The Minister for Social and Family Development reported that median gross monthly income for employed residents with disabilities aged 15 to 64 rose from over $2,500 in 2019 to more than $4,000 in 2025, based on the Ministry of Manpower's Comprehensive Labour Force Survey. Concurrently, the proportion of these individuals in professional, managerial, executive, or technician roles increased from 34% to 54% during the same period. Additionally, the 2023 National Council of Social Service Disability Inclusion Panel Study indicated that 21.6% of persons with disabilities aged 18 and above participated in training programmes in the preceding year.

results.title: Update on Fraudulent Transactions of Dealer Who Took Vehicles Without Payment
results.summary: The alleged car dealer was charged on 8 November 2024 and 2 April 2026 for 12 counts of cheating under section 420 of the Penal Code, with prosecution ongoing. While investigations into fraudulent intent are complex, the Police have deployed levers to prevent reoffending, including freezing suspected bank accounts under the Criminal Procedure Code and imposing bail conditions prohibiting new offences. Additionally, the Police collaborated with relevant Government agencies and private companies to block the dealer and his entities from conducting motoring transactions or posting advertisements on motoring platforms. These measures remain in effect while evidence is gathered to prove fraudulent intent rather than genuine business failure.

results.title: Vehicular Noise Reduction Measures for Residential Buildings Adjacent to Expressways
results.summary: Since July 2016, the National Environment Agency mandates Land Traffic Noise Impact Assessments for new residential developments near existing noise hotspots like expressways, ensuring mitigation at the planning stage. For existing buildings such as HDB flats at Whampoa adjacent to the CTE, the Land Transport Authority collaborates with agencies to implement feasible noise reduction measures including low-noise pavement, noise barriers, and vegetation. The Minister did not confirm setting new vehicle noise thresholds at residential facades or specific protocols for installing vegetation/panels if thresholds are breached.

results.title: Work-from-home Contingency Plans for Businesses and Companies Amid Rising Global Energy Costs
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower stated that the Government will elaborate on the fuel situation in Ministerial Statements on 7 April 2026. Currently, there are no plans to direct businesses and companies to review work-from-home requirements despite rising global energy prices. The response does not indicate any immediate regulatory changes or specific contingency measures for work-from-home arrangements at this time.
